topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The blue light of the phone screen felt like a physical weight pressing against Elara’s palm. She scrolled through her feed, the usual stream of trivial updates offering no solace. Then, a notification popped up: a photo of Professor Albright, smiling brightly, captioned with a quote about intellectual curiosity. Elara blinked, rereading the caption. It was a picture taken in a brightly lit office, and the angle of the shot was undeniably the same angle she had taken of him during the last seminar. A strange knot tightened in her stomach, a dull ache spreading through her chest. She quickly minimized the app, her fingers hovering over the screen as if afraid to touch it again. The silence of her cramped dorm room suddenly felt loud, amplifying the drumming in her ears.

[story 2]

Mark stared at the notification, his jaw tightening until his molars ached. It was a picture of Ms. Davies, a candid shot from a recent school event, and beneath it, a comment from her—a direct reply to someone else's post, seemingly directed at him. His shoulders slumped, a slow, heavy surrender settling over him. He didn't move to open the message thread. Instead, he picked up his pen, the cheap plastic cool against his skin, and began methodically sketching geometric shapes on a scrap of notebook paper, the movements slow and deliberate, as if trying to physically etch the tension out of his limbs. The fluorescent lights of the library hummed with an irritating persistence.

[story 3]

"Are you still there, Alex?" The message appeared on his laptop screen. It was Professor Evans. Alex felt a sudden, sharp dryness in his throat. He hadn't seen anything from the professor in weeks, just the usual dry assignments and impersonal emails. This unsolicited glimpse into the professor's personal digital space made him want to physically lean back in his chair until the cheap plastic groaned in protest. He rubbed his temples, the pressure behind his eyes intensifying. He closed his eyes, counting to ten, a futile attempt to reset the frantic rhythm of his pulse.
